other_observers: There were 3 other observers looking at the bird of which I did not know.; I spotted the bird and alerted those around me to help get confirmation of what I was looking at.
specific_location: small group of trees adjacent to feeders at Discovery Science Museum.
description: Bird was small finch body shape with small, short yellow bill. I would estimate that it was only 4-5 inches in body height. The bird was a pale snowy color with faded, minimal streaking on the sides. The crown of the bird had a clear bright red crown patch. The facial markings were pale and whitish with little in the way of markings. His chin had a small black patch. The breast of the bird was snowy white with no other streaking, the undertail coverts were snowy white with one small brown streak. Based on the position of the bird I could not see its rump and flew it flew away before I could examine its back feathering further. Its bill was noticeably shorter than the common redpolls around it from a subjective comparative standpoint.Behaviors: The bird really did not exhibit any specific behaviors other than to remain perched in group of trees that were parallel to feeders at the Discovery Science Museum in Fort Collins, CO. The bird was in the middle of group of other redpolls numbering around 20. I was able to watch the bird for about 3-4 minutes before it left the area.Call: NAPlumage: winter
